ODE TO STAR TREK…     (2.28.2026) - rwb

If Shatner had stayed skinny,
If Nimoy could live forever,
If Scotty could fix any,
They still could not stop Woke!

Picard defeated the Assimilated,
Data his own ego choked,
These crews every enemy humiliated,
But they still could not stop Woke!

Phasers set on stun,
Photon torpedoes away,
Break Prime Directive’s run,
But they could not stop Woke!

Cancel a season,
Design a better starship,
Even shut reason’s lip,
But they could not stop Woke!

Finally, a child of four,
Said King Star Trek has no clothes,
If it’s Woke… it’s poor,
Q then erased it as a very bad joke!

So then what, pray tell, and why…
Is highest game afoot to follow?
As is said…For the world is hollow
And I have touched the sky!

So, logically, Spock if not Kirk… would intuit,
Class M Planet Earth at 4.5 billion years old,
Oracle AIs ancient & perhaps imprudent,
Super powered wokism to prevent going bold…

To keep star dust,
Believing it is mud, not essence of stars,
To keep them bust,
And not going FTL, restless… past Mars!

ATTN: Enemies of raucous humanity, nothing can stop good from winning!
Not mind-melting wokism, nor ancient wars between AIs white and black,
Granted, humans may pause and time out… on some seventh innings,
But then, re-energized,  hit home runs, with God’s wind at their backs!

To real humans, Captain Kirk will always be skinny,
Spock, in our minds, lives in sublime logic forever,
Bones heals all and Scotty fixes brokenness any,
And nothing, nothing stops good from winning!

replies.author: @frocat5163
replies.text:
@PapaEmeritusII 
*"Ridiculous assumptions."*

First, I apologize if my evaluation of your comment was incorrect. In my defense, however, your exact words were, _"The term neurodivergent is more buzzword modern age silliness."_ 

Nothing about that statement implies the term is being overused to justify bad behavior. It's a definitive statement in which you directly said the term "neurodivergent" is simply a "buzzword" and should be lumped in with other "modern age silliness." 

Within this context, i.e. on a Nerdrotic video, a channel where people routinely decry all manner of things to be "fake," someone calling the term 'neurodivergent' "buzzword modern age silliness" with no additional context would be right in line with the other declarations by Nerdrotic viewers. So, again, if that wasn't your intention, I apologize.

*" I just mean everyone is hyper focused on all this adhd, neurodivergent stuff nowadays... Most of them completely undiagnosed!"*

As someone who *IS* diagnosed with ASD (technically I was diagnosed with Asperger's Syndrome in the mid '90s before it was moved under the umbrella of ASD in 2013; if I were diagnosed today it would likely be 'Level-One Autism'), I'm glad there's more focus on it. Making people aware of neurodivergence leads to better treatment, care, and interactions for individuals with those disorders. I have a son who was diagnosed with Combined ADHD as a 7-year-old, and I've watched him struggle with it throughout his time in school, despite treatment and support (he's a senior in high school this year). As a result, I tend to be a bit defensive when people dismiss ASD and ADHD as if they're not real issues that can have serious impact on a person's daily life. 

That being said, I am incredibly annoyed when someone who hasn't been diagnosed with something attempts to use the condition as a justification for their bad behavior. 

*"I know alot of people in real life that wont shut up about it, like its their only defining character trait."*

One of the characteristics of ASD is hyper-focus on very specific topics. If you encounter someone with ASD who has fixated on ASD as one of their topics of interest, they will absolutely talk about it nonstop as if it's their only defining character trait...because, in a way, _it is._ 

As a kid, I had hyper-fixation on a few topics, two of which were dinosaurs and aircraft. By age 6, I could recite mountains of facts about hundreds of dinosaur species. I even corrected my first grade teacher in the middle of class one day for mispronouncing 'Diplodocus'. At that age, I could also tell you every aircraft model flown by the US military, as well as recite facts about them that no six-year-old should know. And I've never really lost interest in those things. From age 4 until I had to pick a college to attend, I went back and forth with myself on whether to become a paleontologist or a pilot (I settled on pilot). I *STILL* devour mountains of data on dinosaurs, though.

But, again, you are absolutely correct that there are people who won't shut up about being 'neurodivergent' when they've never been diagnosed...kind of like the people who choose to be gluten-free and won't shut up about it, but have never been diagnosed with celiac disease. 

*""You wouldnt understand because youre neurotypical and im neurodivergent...Oh thats because Im neurodivergent!...I cant do that because of my ADHD!" Etc."*

As I said elsewhere in these comments, it's been my experience that most people who use 'neurodivergent' as a justification for bad behavior are people who haven't actually been diagnosed. There are certainly times I've told someone that something I've done that they didn't understand was because I'm neurodivergent and they're neurotypical, but never as a crutch or justification for being an @sshole, simply as an explanation. I'm generally able to mask my behaviors and just fit in with "normies," but when I get tired or I'm "peopled out," it becomes much harder.

author: @smeissner328
text:
The biggest problem I have is that this isn't **science** fiction anymore. Star Trek used to care about realism and exploring possible futures. New Trek has abandoned scientific speculation in favor of juvenile "lol so random!" attempts at humor. 

A prime example is Darem vomiting glitter. Why glitter? Is his body's reaction in accordance with a scientific concept relating to his species? Or does it only happen because a writer with zero scientific acumen thought "haha, glitter vomit" ? And the entirety of the sentient "hologram" character is an affront to science. Absolutely nothing about it makes rational sense.

This isn't science fiction, it's a crappy sitcom.

replies.author: @Gimil38
replies.text: The glitter puke is one of the lesser problems of this show and the greater whole of what is going on.

replies.author: @smeissner328
replies.text: @Gimil38 Of course it is. I just chose it as an example because the difference in writing abilities between old and new Trek is very easy to see in that scene. The writers of older Trek shows created real science fiction, tackling serious "what ifs" in a mature and grounded way. The new writers don't have the maturity and intellectual understanding to write real sci-fi even if they wanted to.

replies.text:
Nah, they just make a new series and right at the start it turns out to have taken place in another alternate timeline and they have to find a way to close the door to the alternate universe before it basically causes a real Burn and destroys the Prime universe.

Heck, they could even take the political commentary route and have all of Kurtzman Trek be a simulation run by a rogue group of Species 8472 with the intent of destroying the Federation from within by injecting identity politics into their society. The Burn was a simple way to keep the simulation small since nobody could travel very far. Of course, that's what happened in our world in 2011, right after Occupy Wall Street. Somebody injected identity politics into our society and it has nearly destroyed us by now.

Anyways, the real Starfleet could discover the simulation and go in to rescue any of the Kurtzman characters we actually do like so they can start their long journey to rehabilitation. Imagine, somewhere in the second season that tiny Romulan officially joins the bridge crew of a real star ship as an ensign, showing the appropriate respect for authority. Maybe we'll save the chancellor of the War College as well, and that Betazed president. They will have to re-learn some masculinity before they can lead again though.

The Kurtzman EMH was a bad copy of the real one, riddled with bugs, which explains his literal damage. The real EMH has long since disappeared. We definitely don't want that guy back after this. As for Holly Hunter... a baby has to learn how to crawl before it can learn how to walk, and Holly Hunter has to learn how to sit before she can leave the mental institution. And tie her shoelaces, because yuck!

replies.author: @builder396
replies.text:
Actually if you go back a bit Isaac, or more specifically the Kaylon, have a similar backstory to the Geth, AI that were developed for servitude and gained sapience over time, with the difference being that the Kaylon rebelled against being casually tortured, while the Geth defended themselves from being whiped out by their creators, who started to fear them, which are still similarly understandable.....though the Geth stopped short of committing their own genocide, which is a considerable point in their favor. Then both became hardcore isolationists who shot down anything and everything that entered their territory.
But I still respect the Orville with making things a little more original, because it diverges from the Geth story from that point onward. Where Legion sought out Shepard specifically to fight the Reapers Isaac was there to judge organic life and if deemed hostile compromise their defenses, which led to a whole overarching plot of Isaac getting pranked on leading to war. Talk about a domino effect. 
You can definitely have parallels as long as you do your own thing. But Kurtzman Treks only "own thing" is playing with crayons, and literally dumbing down the plot into one episode thats apparently geared towards toddlers.
